title: “A Person Should Be in Love Most of the Time.” An[other] Ode to Grace Paley
description: I first started reading Grace Paley in the weeks after my second novel was published, as I sought refuge from refreshing Goodreads, from waiting for the Booker Prize Committee to phone. Reading the five-page piece “Love,” and rereading it many
hook: Reading Grace Paley as refuge from obsessing over prizes—how a writer's work becomes shelter from her own ambition.

title: The Speculative Empathy and Radical Hope of Disclosure Day
description: For most of Spielberg’s career, science fiction has been the mechanism through which he learns and teaches empathy. But in Disclosure Day, empathy itself is the speculative leap. The genre function is inverted: science fiction stops speculating about aliens and starts speculating about us. Aliens are ordinary. Human compassion is extraordinary. The sci-fi is us.
hook: Spielberg stops imagining aliens and starts imagining us: how science fiction becomes a tool for understanding human vulnerability.

title: Restaurant Review: Zoli
description: Zoli, which now anchors the sprawling East Williamsburg art space Amant, earns its experimental flourishes by delivering pleasure at every turn.
hook: A restaurant that earns the right to be weird by making food that actually tastes good—pleasure first, experimentation second.
